title: AWS Organizations and Multi-Account Governance
description:
## Managing Many Accounts

At Professional scale you don't manage one account — you manage **dozens or hundreds**. **AWS Organizations** is the service that groups accounts under a single umbrella for governance and consolidated billing. This is the backbone of the "organizational complexity" exam domain.

### Key concepts

| Concept | What it is |
|---|---|
| **Management account** | The single account that *creates* the organization. Owns billing, invites/creates member accounts, and applies policies. Keep it minimal — run no workloads here. |
| **Member account** | Every other account in the org. Isolation boundary for environments, teams, or workloads. |
| **Organizational Unit (OU)** | A container that groups accounts hierarchically (e.g. `Prod`, `Dev`, `Sandbox`, `Security`). Policies attach to OUs and cascade down. |
| **Consolidated billing** | One payer (management account) for all members, with **combined volume discounts** and shared Reserved Instance / Savings Plan benefits. |
| **Service Control Policy (SCP)** | An organization **guardrail** that defines the **maximum** permissions for accounts/OUs it applies to. |

### SCPs: ceilings, never grants

> An **SCP never grants permissions.** It defines the **permission ceiling**. Effective permissions = the *intersection* of what IAM policies allow **and** what SCPs allow.

If an SCP doesn't allow an action, **no principal in that account — not even the account's own administrator or root user — can perform it.** That's what makes SCPs the right tool for non-negotiable, org-wide guardrails (e.g. "no one may turn off CloudTrail," "only approved Regions may be used").

### Where the governance services fit

| Service | Role |
|---|---|
| **AWS Control Tower** | Sets up and governs a **multi-account landing zone** automatically — OUs, baseline guardrails, log archive/audit accounts, and **Account Factory** for provisioning new accounts. Built **on top of** Organizations. |
| **IAM Identity Center** (formerly AWS SSO) | Centralized **workforce sign-on** across all accounts, with **permission sets** and integration to an external IdP. |

> Mental model: **Organizations** = the account hierarchy + billing + SCP guardrails. **Control Tower** = an opinionated, automated way to stand that up well. **IAM Identity Center** = how humans log in across all of it.
